comments.text: I love my chickens, feed them a soy free corn free and herb diet with some good protein and organ meats and my oh myyyyyy do they lay and are the most beautiful feathered girls ever!

replies.text: @_jesus_taime_7 not sure I quite understand your comment.
@lizz_ayalaaa au contraire il faut qu'elle mange de l'herbe 🙄

@jojoroussy from what I’ve learned that’s true but only if you feed them raw. Because when they lay they’ll peck at them and then eat them. I personally cook them and then feed them their eggs. We’ll sprinkle in some eggshell here and there it’s a treat and has a good source of calcium. But too much egg shell because of the high calcium can also cause problems. So egg shells here and there. Hope this helps!
@lizz_ayalaaa I thought if you feed their eggs back to them they get a taste for them and eat their own eggs??
@stryder.g you also feed their eggs back to them. I always cook everything some people like giving raw meat and eggs back to the chickens but I’m paranoid lol I’d rather cook it haha
@stryder.g organ meat live livers, gizzards, hearts. I make sure to give them the best quality. I buy chicken from a local farmer that only pasture raises his chickens.
@stryder.g Modesto corn free and soy free! It’s great!
@lizz_ayalaaa can you message me info on what you feed your chickens? 🥹🙏🏻 i would love to feed my chickens better i didn’t know they can eat organ meat
@lizz_ayalaaa what meat and layer do you feed them ?

comments.text: They eat better then us
Am I the only one who doesn’t have chickens? 🥺
How do we buy the eggs from you or not for sale to the public because I’m allergic to wheat and I trust no one. 😩

@stompinnecks ha. No. I would never but thanks for that. 🫶🏼
@charle__ are you vaccinated? If so, then that’s why you have the disease!!

comments.text: @lizz_ayalaaa what meat and layer do you feed them ?
@stryder.g Modesto corn free and soy free! It’s great!
Because they don’t have teeth, the shells add grit to help break down and digest their food
@mayanu_ hahaha
@lizz_ayalaaa can you message me info on what you feed your chickens? 🥹🙏🏻 i would love to feed my chickens better i didn’t know they can eat organ meat
@stryder.g you also feed their eggs back to them. I always cook everything some people like giving raw meat and eggs back to the chickens but I’m paranoid lol I’d rather cook it haha

comments.text: All great ! But missing meat or worms.

replies.text: @tinyhomesteadau a chicken's regular day has them looking for worms and bugs in the dirt. That's why you should have a large grazing area or move them around to fresh areas, every few days.  Not everyone has the ability, but it's the best way....or feed them the worms you bring
@marika8982 he feeds them worms for protein?

@marika8982 you missed the part where it said worms. Look at it again and see if you can spot the word. Sometimes,  we are quick to criticize or analyze things before actually reading and thinking it thru. Look again and you will find
@marika8982 that’s what I was thinking they need some like mealworms or something. That’s what I give my chickens, meal, worms and veggies and all types of seeds

@jojoroussy from what I’ve learned that’s true but only if you feed them raw. Because when they lay they’ll peck at them and then eat them. I personally cook them and then feed them their eggs. We’ll sprinkle in some eggshell here and there it’s a treat and has a good source of calcium. But too much egg shell because of the high calcium can also cause problems. So egg shells here and there. Hope this helps!
@lizz_ayalaaa I thought if you feed their eggs back to them they get a taste for them and eat their own eggs??
@stryder.g you also feed their eggs back to them. I always cook everything some people like giving raw meat and eggs back to the chickens but I’m paranoid lol I’d rather cook it haha
@stryder.g organ meat live livers, gizzards, hearts. I make sure to give them the best quality. I buy chicken from a local farmer that only pasture raises his chickens.
@stryder.g Modesto corn free and soy free! It’s great!

replies.text: @tinyhomesteadau a chicken's regular day has them looking for worms and bugs in the dirt. That's why you should have a large grazing area or move them around to fresh areas, every few days.  Not everyone has the ability, but it's the best way....or feed them the worms you bring
